_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-3 Nausea & Vomiting (Revised 10/24/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-3: Nausea & Vomiting — MDHHS Approved 10/24/25, Reviewed 2025

sections.title: Nausea & Vomiting
points: Consider underlying causes of nausea and vomiting (e.g., stroke, trauma, cardiac) and evaluate according to appropriate protocol
Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age): utilize MI MEDIC for appropriate fluid bolus; ondansetron does not appear on MI MEDIC — use dosing listed in this protocol
Isopropyl alcohol: consider allowing patient to inhale vapor from isopropyl alcohol wipe 3 times every 15 minutes as tolerated

For patients > 30 kg (adult and pediatric) that are NOT actively vomiting: administer ondansetron (Zofran) 4 mg ODT one time (availability and licensure level per MCA selection); CONTRAINDICATION: Phenylketonuria (PKU)

For signs of dehydration: administer NS or LR IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill — refer to Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol); Adults: up to 1 liter; Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg
Hypotensive patients should receive additional IV/IO fluid boluses as indicated: Adults repeat to maximum 2 liters; Pediatrics repeat 20 mL/kg to maximum 40 mL/kg

Administer ondansetron IV/IM if ODT not already administered OR if patient vomited post ODT administration (per MCA selection — may be Specialist skill); Adults: 4 mg IV/IM; Pediatrics: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IM, maximum dose 4 mg

May repeat ondansetron ONCE (may be Specialist skill if selected above) after 15 minutes if nausea/vomiting persist — second dose must be IV/IM; Adults: 4 mg IV/IM; Pediatrics: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IM, maximum 4 mg; total maximum ondansetron dose (all routes) for adults or pediatrics: 8 mg

Consider diphenhydramine when previous medications have been ineffective or are contraindicated; Adults: 12.5–25 mg IV/IM, maximum dose 25 mg; Pediatrics (> 2 years of age AND > 12 kg): 1.0 mg/kg IV, maximum dose 25 mg — NOTE: diphenhydramine for nausea/vomiting does not appear on MI MEDIC

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must indicate whether ODT ondansetron is included and which licensure level may administer
MCA must indicate which licensure level may administer IV/IM ondansetron

key_drugs.name: Ondansetron (Zofran) ODT
key_drugs.dose: 4 mg ODT — adults and pediatrics > 30 kg not actively vomiting; one time dose
key_drugs.indication: Nausea and vomiting — patient not actively vomiting and weight > 30 kg
key_drugs.contraindications: Phenylketonuria (PKU); actively vomiting patients; weight ≤ 30 kg
key_drugs.side_effects: Headache, constipation, QT prolongation (rare at standard doses)
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT or Specialist
key_drugs.notes: Ondansetron does not appear on MI MEDIC. If patient vomits after ODT, may administer IV/IM dose.

key_drugs.name: Ondansetron IV/IM
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 4 mg IV/IM; Pediatrics: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IM, maximum 4 mg; total max all routes: 8 mg
key_drugs.indication: Nausea and vomiting when ODT not administered or patient vomited post ODT
key_drugs.contraindications: Phenylketonuria (PKU); already received 8 mg ondansetron total
key_drugs.notes: May repeat once after 15 minutes if nausea/vomiting persist.

key_drugs.name: Diphenhydramine
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 12.5–25 mg IV/IM, max 25 mg; Pediatrics (> 2 y/o AND > 12 kg): 1.0 mg/kg IV, max 25 mg
key_drugs.indication: Nausea and vomiting when ondansetron ineffective or contraindicated
key_drugs.contraindications: Children < 2 years of age; weight ≤ 12 kg in pediatrics
key_drugs.notes: Does not appear on MI MEDIC.

deterioration_flags: Signs of dehydration (dry mucous membranes, tachycardia, poor skin turgor) — initiate IV/IO (Specialist skill) and fluid bolus
Hypotension despite fluids — consider underlying cause; refer to Shock-Treatment Protocol
Consider stroke, MI, or toxic ingestion as underlying etiology — evaluate and refer to appropriate protocol
scope_notes: ODT Ondansetron: scope per MCA selection — EMT or Specialist
IV/IM Ondansetron: scope per MCA selection — Specialist
IV/IO access and fluid bolus: Specialist skill
